changes_made: Changed k8s_apiserver_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The control PM-31-b is applicable to Kubernetes as it relates to continuous monitoring, which is supported by audit logging and configuration tracking in K8s. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but 'k8s_apiserver_logging_enabled' was too generic and needed to be more specific to audit logging. The functions are technically accurate and map well to K8s security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names specify the type of logging, e.g., 'audit', to avoid ambiguity
title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy - Establishing [ assignment: organization-defined frequencies...
step1_reasoning: The control PM-31-b focuses on continuous monitoring strategies, which in the context of Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled and properly configured to capture relevant events. This includes ensuring audit logs are retained for a specific period and monitoring changes to network policies. Additionally, API server logging should be enabled to track access and changes.
